Petition For Order Construing Trust Instrument And Instructing The Trustees
Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sara Eisenberg for the Trustees of the SF War Memorial and Performing Arts Center. Todd Toral & Jesse Medlong for (petitioner) San Francisco Posts of the American Legion.

Ms. Eisenberg stated that the case is ready to be argued and submitted. Mr. Medlong stated that the petition is not barred, that the equitable defense of Laches does not apply, that this is a dispute about the purpose of a trust which is to provide aid and support to those who defend us, cited 16460, that the trust has always been interpreted to include organizations who provided service, that the Veteran's Service Organizations Board has blocked a resolution, that their duty requires them to understand the trust and that the purpose of the trust is to serve veterans. Ms. Eisenberg stated that there are a lot of deep emotions, that this is a straight forward case of trust interpretation regarding non member VSO's, that the letter from 1933 stated the understanding of VSO's, cited and read 1331 & 1340, that the historical evidence is strong, that it is in favor of the stand taken by the board, that the Veteran's of the Mexican War (VMW), etc., were considered surplussage, that there is no indication that VMW was ever given space in the building, that the legion membership has specific criteria "of like membership", that the entire provisions of the trust need to be read, that the statute of limitations starts with the 2009 Swords to Ploughshares, that the probate court can apply laches, that there is no dispute that the posts knew about the challenge in 2009, cited the Potter case, that there was a demand letter requesting the entire 2nd and 3rd floors and that they will seek usage of the entire building and that it is the duty of the trustees to apply as directed. Mr. Medlong stated that the historical record is not decisive, that a 1933 record suggests that a VSO not part of any of the membership organizations, Sons and Daughters of the American Revolution, were in the building from the beginning and that the hearsay regarding the phone book is not fully briefed. The Court asked about the trust interpretation. Mr. Medlong stated that we should not disregard the historical record, that there is evidence of organizations in the building, that the trust has allowed in Daughters of Confederacy, Royal British War Group and Russian Veterans and that the historical record was not that strict. The Court stated that there is a broader context expanding over a greater period of time. Mr. Medlong talked about a close reading of examples provided by the board regarding "of like membership', that rooms were only to be used for meetings and social events. Mr. Medlong stated that the Veterans were to receive comfort, aid and support, that the beneficiaries were harmed in 2009 by the refusal to install Swords to Ploughshares, that the beneficiaries of this trust, the city's Veterans, are seeking interpretation, that there have been negotiations and that they are unaware as to why the board of trustees did not bring their own petition. The Court stated that the final point argued narrow vs. expansiveness. Mr. Medlong stated that they do not encourage expansiveness, that the historical record contradicts the record and that it is for organizations that provide the right services to Veterans. Ms. Eisenberg stated that the trustees did not promise to change construction. The Court gave the parties until November 12, 2015 to submit post hearing briefs and the petition for order construing trust instrument and instructing trustees will be under submission as of that date. Subsequent to the hearing the parties were also instructed to provide proposed orders and any objections to the proposed orders by the same date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Court Reporter Hanna Kim, CSR #13083, 415-300-0469 (Reported)
